algorithms.title: Acute Ischemic Stroke — Prehospital and Emergency Department Management
algorithms.description: End-to-end pathway from stroke recognition to reperfusion decision, including IV thrombolysis and thrombectomy.
steps: Stroke recognized (Cincinnati/FAST positive) — activate stroke alert and notify receiving stroke center.
If glucose <50 mg/dL → administer IV dextrose, reassess (may be stroke mimic).
Acquire 12-lead ECG, vital signs, SpO₂. Maintain airway if needed.
On arrival at stroke center: non-contrast CT head (MANDATORY before any reperfusion therapy).
CT shows intracranial hemorrhage → STOP: no tPA; neurosurgical consultation.
CT shows no hemorrhage, ischemic stroke confirmed or highly suspected.
Determine symptom onset (last known normal time).
Within 4.5-hour window AND no tPA contraindications?
YES → Check BP: must be <185/110 mmHg before tPA.
→ If BP >185/110: IV labetalol 10–20 mg over 1–2 min (repeat once) or nicardipine 5 mg/hr infusion (titrate up).
→ BP <185/110 achieved → Administer tPA: 0.9 mg/kg IV (max 90 mg), 10% bolus over 1 min, remainder over 60 min.
→ Maintain BP <180/105 during and for ≥24 hours after tPA (reduce by ~15% in first 24 h).
Large-vessel occlusion suspected? Obtain CTA head and neck.
LVO confirmed AND onset ≤6 hours (or ≤24 h with perfusion imaging showing penumbra) → proceed to mechanical thrombectomy (do not delay tPA).
Outside 4.5-hour window OR tPA contraindicated → permissive hypertension up to 220/120 (if no hemorrhage/cardiac ischemia); if LVO and within window, thrombectomy.
Admit to stroke unit for continued monitoring and care.
